title: RAG AI
author: Priya
content:
Retrieval Augmented generation is an AI model use to simplify search response to user's query.It uses LLMs to generate most accurate, relevant response by using its vector databases and also by using other external data sources that may be some external text file, database or some other relevant data source.

Search through LLMs without RAG -> When a user's text prompt received by LLMs than based on what the available data it has or the limited knowledge llm has , it prompt with usable response to user's query that may not be must factual or complete.It uses its static training data and don't extend its search through other external sources to output query.
It's limitations
- It gives response based on what knowledge it has and don't widen it's search to look into external sources for most reliable, thorough response that should be given.
- If it don't have any exact knowledge on the questions being asked it does word to word mapping in its database to respond which may not even much accurate.
With RAG
-> It works in 2 phases -
1. Retrieval = Based on users query , it looks for all the available sources matching with users query , also create embeddings(with Embedding language Model) by converting users query in numerical datasets and search for it in its vector databases.
2.Generation = When all results r retrieved than it uses prompt engineering to augment all those data sources along with users query and pass it as input to LL.M .Than LLM use those sources to give text based response to users on their query.

Foundation Model(FM) Vs RAG


Foundation ModelRetrieval Augmented generation
FM r some LLM based ai APIs that performs search over some general, unlabelled datasets. Results of searches through FM r not that clear and concise as it performs searches on a set of data which has some general text based data not so specific to pick exact response to user's query. RAG performs advance level search unlike FMs .They utilises all possible external deta source, vector databases to take out most proper , accurate response.
it works on static ,pre-trained data .eg... used in Gemini, GPT, or Claude and do not provide sufficient details required for an accurate response. It searches through external data sets and does not limit itself to a pre-defined set of knowledge feeded on LLMs but rather uses smart search techniques using embedding language model to give most preferred response to users.
They r more conventional,uses traditional search practices.They r more advance and give proper search results with citations to reference where data is taken from and produces results far better than that of traditional FMs

Corporates can create their own RAG by using it simple retrieve and augmentation principal and use numerical based order through vector search to generate relevant responses.Eg...To answer human resource questions for an organization. If an employee searches, "How much annual leave do I have?" the system will retrieve annual leave policy documents alongside the individual employee's past leave record. These specific documents will be returned because they are highly-relevant to what the employee has input. The relevancy was calculated and established using mathematical vector calculations.

Semantic search Vs RAG search


  • Semantic Search uses elastic search technique to find contexual meaning of data being asked rather than just its literal meaning.It draws out more clear response and don not just work on word to word mappings instead take out actual relevant data using vector search embeddings and proposed a better response with clear notion and understanding 1 can derive from it.
  • Semantic search enhances RAG search by giving more knowledge intensive and simplistic response. RAG search uses traditional search approach of looking over all the available data source and pass all those data sources to LLMs to refine their search.But semantic search instead of return search results with available data sources as incase of RAG , it generates more meaningful text response from those data sources. So, RAG combinedly with semantic search produce more reasonable, clear , descriptive response.
